name: AI accelerator silicon (GPU + ASIC)
severityNote: NVIDIA allocation-gated; custom ASIC ramps lag
description: GPUs (NVIDIA H/B/Rubin, AMD MI300/400) and hyperscaler-custom ASICs (Google TPU, AWS Trainium, Meta MTIA, Microsoft Maia) are the silicon endpoint of the whole AI capex chain. Aggregate AI accelerator wafer-equivalent demand exceeds TSMC's combined N3/N3P/CoWoS-coupled output, so allocation is the operative variable rather than spot-price.
stackPosition: Tape-out at TSMC N3P/N3 (or Samsung 2nm-gen for some custom) → wafer fab → die sort → ship to CoWoS for HBM stack attach → known-good-package → board assembly at OEM (Foxconn, Wistron, Quanta) → SXM module or OAM → server → rack → datacenter. Every accelerator passes through TSMC + CoWoS + HBM — three of the most constrained nodes in the entire stack — and the silicon endpoint inherits all of those constraints plus its own (mask sets, validation, software stack).

supplyStructure.name: Google TPU (v5/v6/v7)
supplyStructure.note: Largest custom ASIC volume; AVGO is the listed beneficiary

supplyStructure.name: Microsoft Maia / Meta MTIA / Tesla Dojo
supplyStructure.note: All ramping but small share of total

companies.name: Tesla Dojo / OpenAI silicon team
companies.note: Hyperscaler in-house designs not externalized; strategic-not-merchant

insight: The accelerator-silicon market is bimodal: NVIDIA-allocation-gated for merchant supply, and custom-ASIC-pacing-gated for the hyperscalers ramping their own silicon. The investable wedges are (1) NVIDIA at any cyclical drawdown — the moat is software more than silicon, and (2) the ASIC design-service oligopoly Broadcom + Marvell, which captures the hyperscaler-custom dollar without taking technology risk. The widow-maker is small-cap AI silicon pure plays — most are pre-revenue, behind on software, and structurally cannot reach NVIDIA's per-watt economics. Watch China-domestic accelerator path (Huawei Ascend, Cambricon) for the geopolitical edge.

debates.question: Does NVIDIA's datacenter market share drop below 75% by end of 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Likely as custom ASIC mix climbs; NVIDIA's software moat keeps the floor near 70%

debates.question: Does AMD's Instinct revenue exceed $25B in fiscal 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Plausible if MI400 ramp + OpenAI / Meta commits hold; consensus around $18B

debates.question: Does Intel reach a credible foundry-customer milestone (TSM-class qualification) by 2027?
debates.currentBelief: 18A is the binary event; recent yield disclosures encouraging but not definitive
